title: Walnut cake
subtitle: A tender sponge with walnuts folded in and a walnut cream icing
excerpt: Walnut cake is a lightly spiced sponge with ground and chopped walnuts worked through the batter, layered with a walnut-studded cream cheese or buttercream icing. The walnuts give the crumb a slight bitterness and a faintly coarse texture that reads differently from a plain sponge. Best made a day ahead so the icing has time to set.
sourceNotes: Walnut cakes appear in British baking from the late nineteenth century onward, when walnuts became widely available. The form here follows the standard British layer cake formula documented in domestic cookery manuals. The cream cheese icing variant is a twentieth-century adaptation.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unfilled sponges. Assemble after defrosting.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the sponges a day ahead; assemble and refrigerate overnight. Remove 30 minutes before serving.

content.text: Walnut cake has ground walnuts replacing part of the flour and chopped walnuts folded through the batter, which gives the sponge a slightly dense, pleasantly coarse texture and a mild bitterness. The icing is a cream cheese or buttercream spiked with walnut pieces. The assembled cake is better after a night in the fridge, when the flavours settle.

items.prepNote: 50 g ground fine; 50 g roughly chopped

content.text: Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Grease and line two 20 cm sandwich tins. Beat the butter and brown sugar until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well. Fold in the flour, ground walnuts, and milk until just combined, then fold in the chopped walnuts. Divide between the two tins and bake for 22 to 25 minutes. The sponges are done when they spring back when pressed and have pulled away from the sides. Cool completely on a wire rack before icing.

Beat the butter until smooth, then beat in the cream cheese. Sift in the icing sugar and mix until the icing is thick and holds a peak. Do not overbeat; cream cheese icing splits if worked too hard. Sandwich the two sponges with about half the icing, then spread the remainder on top. Press walnut halves around the top edge. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before slicing.
